content:
@Tamir Duberstein @Benno Lossin , let's use this thread for the preparation on the in-kernel document explaining strict_provenance (and maybe including explaining provenance as well).

A few things that I think we need to cover in the document:

  • We need to explain the benefits of using strict_provenance API, does it affect optimization? What kind of mistakes can it prevent, etc.
    • We may also need to explain why the concept "provenance" is an important part in Rust's aliasing(?) model. IIUC, "provenance" gives the compiler justification to do certain optimization, it's going to be good to show this with some examples.
  • List a few examples that which API should be used in int<->ptr cases, and these can be useful for people to follow if they want to do a particular conversion.

Boqun Feng said:

  • We need to explain the benefits of using strict_provenance API, does it affect optimization? What kind of mistakes can it prevent, etc.

At this time there is no difference, but that is expected to eventually change once there is better LLVM support
For (incorrect) optimization examples, the "How 3 (seemingly) correct optimizations..." section of this blog has a good small demo https://www.ralfj.de/blog/2020/12/14/provenance.html
